---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: df4b1dd0b749dacd29338fcfb65c1cc4874097cb
findings:
  - { id: judged-dc-source-vocab-gap, kind: judged, text: "dc-4 adds a third enumeration source — specs read from the default branch's own tree at its resolved ref (.verdi/specs/active/ and archive/) — that is neither a local design ref nor a remote-tracking design ref, yet dc-3 declares Source as an always-populated enum of exactly {local, remote, both}, the two ref sources parent dc-5 names. Parent dc-5's obligation that 'each entry disclosed by source' therefore has no defined value for default-branch-tree entries: forcing them into 'local' or 'remote' would misstate their provenance (they come from the resolved default-branch ref, typically refs/remotes/origin/HEAD, not a design ref at all), and no decision declares how these entries satisfy dc-5's per-entry source disclosure or exempts them from it. This is an undeclared gap between dc-3's closed vocabulary and dc-4's added source, measured against the parent's disclosure decision. (confidence 0.45)", target_ref: spec/workbench-directory }
sweep_provenance: { adr_corpus_digest: sha256:37517e5f3dc66819f61f5a7bb8ace1921282415f10551d2defa5c3eb0985b570, decisions_scanned: [spec/ref-index#dc-1, spec/ref-index#dc-2, spec/ref-index#dc-3, spec/ref-index#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-1, spec/workbench-directory#dc-2, spec/workbench-directory#dc-3, spec/workbench-directory#dc-4, spec/workbench-directory#dc-5] }

judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"dc-source-vocab-gap\",\"text\":\"dc-4 adds a third enumeration source — specs read from the default branch's own tree at its resolved ref (.verdi/specs/active/ and archive/) — that is neither a local design ref nor a remote-tracking design ref, yet dc-3 declares Source as an always-populated enum of exactly {local, remote, both}, the two ref sources parent dc-5 names. Parent dc-5's obligation that 'each entry disclosed by source' therefore has no defined value for default-branch-tree entries: forcing them into 'local' or 'remote' would misstate their provenance (they come from the resolved default-branch ref, typically refs/remotes/origin/HEAD, not a design ref at all), and no decision declares how these entries satisfy dc-5's per-entry source disclosure or exempts them from it. This is an undeclared gap between dc-3's closed vocabulary and dc-4's added source, measured against the parent's disclosure decision.\",\"confidence\":0.45,\"target\":\"spec/workbench-directory\"}]}\n```" }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dc-source-vocab-gap** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: dc-4 adds a third enumeration source — specs read from the default branch's own tree at its resolved ref (.verdi/specs/active/ and archive/) — that is neither a local design ref nor a remote-tracking design ref, yet dc-3 declares Source as an always-populated enum of exactly {local, remote, both}, the two ref sources parent dc-5 names. Parent dc-5's obligation that 'each entry disclosed by source' therefore has no defined value for default-branch-tree entries: forcing them into 'local' or 'remote' would misstate their provenance (they come from the resolved default-branch ref, typically refs/remotes/origin/HEAD, not a design ref at all), and no decision declares how these entries satisfy dc-5's per-entry source disclosure or exempts them from it. This is an undeclared gap between dc-3's closed vocabulary and dc-4's added source, measured against the parent's disclosure decision. (confidence 0.45)
